2605.06333.title: TinyBayes: Closed-Form Bayesian Inference via Jacobi Prior for Real-Time Image Classification on Edge Devices
2605.06333.paper_summary: Cocoa (Theobroma cacao) is a critical cash crop for millions of smallholder farmers in West Africa, where Cocoa Swollen Shoot Virus Disease (CSSVD) and anthracnose cause devastating yield losses. Automated disease detection from leaf images is essential for early intervention, yet deploying such systems in resource-constrained settings demands models that are small, fast, and require no internet connectivity. Existing edge-deployable plant disease systems rely on end-to-end deep learning without uncertainty quantification, while Bayesian methods for edge devices focus on hardware-level inference architectures rather than agricultural applications. We bridge this gap with TinyBayes, the first framework to combine a closed-form Bayesian classifier with a mobile-grade computer vision pipeline for crop disease detection. Our pipeline uses YOLOv8-Nano (5.9 MB) for lesion localisation, MobileNetV3-Small (3.5 MB) for feature extraction, and the Jacobi prior; a Bayesian method that provides a closed form non-iterative estimators via projection, for the classification. The Jacobi-DMR (Distributed Multinomial Regression) classifier adds only 13.5 KB to the pipeline, bringing the total model size within 9.5 MB, while achieving 78.7% accuracy on the Amini Cocoa Contamination Challenge dataset and enabling end-to-end CPU inference under 150 ms per image. We benchmark against seven classifiers including Random Forest, SVM, Ridge, Lasso, Elastic Net, XGBoost, and Jacobi-GP, and demonstrate that the Jacobi-DMR offers the best trade-off between accuracy, model size, and inference speed for edge deployment. We have proved the asymptotic equivalence and consistency, asymptotic normality and the bias correction of Jacobi-DMR. All data and codes are available here: https://github.com/shouvik-sardar/TinyBayes
2605.06333.paper_summary_zh: 可可（Theobroma cacao）是西非數百萬小農的重要經濟作物，然而可可腫脹芽病毒病（CSSVD）和炭疽病造成了毀滅性的產量損失。從葉片影像自動檢測疾病對於早期干預至關重要，但在資源有限的環境中部署這類系統需要小型、快速且不需要網絡連接的模型。現有的邊緣可部署植物疾病系統依賴於端到端的深度學習，並未進行不確定性量化，而針對邊緣設備的貝葉斯方法則專注於硬體層面的推理架構，而非農業應用。我們通過TinyBayes填補了這一空白，這是第一個將封閉形式的貝葉斯分類器與移動級計算機視覺管道結合的作物疾病檢測框架。我們的管道使用YOLOv8-Nano（5.9 MB）進行病變定位，使用MobileNetV3-Small（3.5 MB）進行特徵提取，並使用Jacobi先驗；這是一種通過投影提供封閉形式非迭代估計的貝葉斯方法，用於分類。Jacobi-DMR（分佈式多項式回歸）分類器僅為管道增加了13.5 KB，使總模型大小控制在9.5 MB以內，同時在Amini可可污染挑戰數據集上達到了78.7%的準確率，並實現了每張影像低於150毫秒的端到端CPU推理。我們對七個分類器進行了基準測試，包括隨機森林、支持向量機、脊回歸、Lasso、彈性網、XGBoost和Jacobi-GP，並證明Jacobi-DMR在準確性、模型大小和推理速度之間提供了最佳的權衡，適合邊緣部署。我們已經證明了Jacobi-DMR的漸近等價性和一致性、漸近正態性以及偏差修正。所有數據和代碼均可在此獲得：https://github.com/shouvik-sardar/TinyBayes
2605.06333.author: Shouvik Sardar et.al.
